titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Mary Smyth Hunter Correspondence
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday March 1, 2018 at 19:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Joan Winterkorn on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2013.

accessrestrict.head: Information About Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Mary Smyth Hunter Correspondence is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Mary Smyth Hunter Correspondence. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Mary Smyth Hunter (1856-1933)
bioghist.p: Mary Smyth Hunter (1856-1933), born in India, married the wealthy coal-mining heir Charles Hunter in 1875. Mary was known for her lavish hospitality at Hill Hall, their country house, and for her patronage of musicians, writers, and artists.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript letters and postcards, mostly signed, addressed to Mary Smyth Hunter and to two of her descendants, Elizabeth Williamson and Phyllis Williamson. Major correspondents include Maurice Baring, Max Beerbohm, Gabriel Fauré, Edmuind Gosse, Edvard Grieg, George Henschel, Henry James, William James, Claude Monet, George Moore, Auguste Rodin, John Singer Sargent, Ethel Smyth, Henry Tonks, Edith Wharton, and Virginia Woolf.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Letters to Mary Hunter, 1895-1935. II. Letters to Elizabeth Williamson, 1915-1940. III. Letters to Phyllis Williamson, 1898-1936.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. consists of letters to Mary Smyth Hunter. Larger files can be found for Henry James, Claude Monet, George Moore, John Singer Sargent, and Ethel Smyth. Other correspondents include Max Beerbohm, Gabriel Fauré, Edmund Gosse, Edvard Grieg, George Henschel, William James, Auguste Rodin, and Edith Wharton.

did.unittitle: Louise Margaret, Princess, Duchess of Connaught

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II. consists of letters to Elizabeth Williamson. There are approximately 275 pieces of correspondence, including both autograph letters and postcards, signed, from Ethel Smyth. There are also letters from Maurice Baring, Ariana Wormeley Curtis, George Moore, Henry Tonks, and Virginia Woolf.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III. consists of letters to Phyllis Williamson. Larger files can be found for George Moore and Henry Tonks. Other correspondents include George Henschel, Joseph M. Hone, and Alfred Sutro.

did.unittitle: Dunedin, [A. Graham Murray (Andrew Graham Murray), Viscount,?]
